title: berlandiera subacaulis
intro: Berlandiera subacaulis, also known as the Mexican tea, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Berlandiera subacaulis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
